---
title: Create a service storefront
description: Present Agent Card capabilities as a human-readable service page without coupling UI to runtime internals.
icon: Store
status: next
verifiedVersion: 0.8.0
verifiedAt: 2026-07-21
product: sdk
---

Hono, Express, Next.js, and TanStack UI projects render a public **service
storefront**. It helps a human inspect what a service offers; it is not an
operations dashboard, marketplace listing, wallet custodian, or substitute for
a protocol-aware buyer. Hono and Express are static/read-only; Next and
TanStack UI add browser invocation.

## Generate the Next workspace surface

Build the repository packages, then run the local CLI so the generated project
uses the same Next contracts as these docs:

```bash
bun run build:packages
bun packages/cli/dist/index.js my-service --adapter=tanstack-ui
# or
bun packages/cli/dist/index.js my-service --adapter=next --ui-preset=folio
```

The public npm CLI is the Stable channel. Do not use an unpinned public CLI to
reproduce this Next page; see [Release channels](/docs/reference/release-channels).

## Public data flow

```text
canonical entrypoint registry
  → Agent Card + health handlers
    → buildServicePageModel(card, { health, baseUrl })
      → shared ServicePageModel
        → static Hono/Express HTML
        → interactive Next/TanStack React
```

`buildServicePageModel()` from `@lucid-agents/http` accepts only public Agent
Card and health data. It preserves or derives:

- agent name, version, description, provider, and documentation URL;
- online/degraded/offline/unknown status;
- registration/signature trust indicators;
- streaming, task, and extension capability summaries;
- public card, health, entrypoint, and task endpoints;
- interfaces, modes, security schemes/requirements, skills, and all public
  endpoint URIs;
- payment method/network/payee/endpoint summaries; and
- per-offering schemas, examples, modes, security, SIWX requirements, prices,
  and invoke/stream URLs.

Storefront code must not inspect private runtime config, payment trackers,
wallet connectors, secrets, internal task records, or adapter-local registries.
That boundary keeps the UI truthful across frameworks.

## Choose and customize a design

The wizard offers three distinct presets:

| Preset | Design |
| --- | --- |
| `dossier` | Existing dark technical dossier with a stable offering rail. |
| `folio` | Light editorial field guide with expressive display type. |
| `console` | Dense dark console optimized for scanning contracts. |

The generated `service-ui.config.ts` is the only visual configuration surface:

```typescript
import { defineServiceUi } from '@lucid-agents/http/service-ui';

export default defineServiceUi({
  preset: 'folio',
  tokens: {
    colors: { accent: '#1859C9' },
    fonts: {
      display: ['Instrument Serif', 'Georgia', 'serif'],
      stylesheetUrl: 'https://fonts.example.com/service.css',
    },
  },
});
```

The API intentionally excludes arbitrary layout and CSS overrides. Unknown
keys, unsafe URLs/font names, invalid hex colors, and inaccessible primary
color combinations fail validation. This keeps custom brands on the same
semantic markup, responsive rules, and public information contract.

## Configure the public origin

The model prefers the first Agent Card `supportedInterfaces` URL unless the
caller supplies `baseUrl`. Configure one canonical external origin and base
path after reverse-proxy rewriting. An internal host such as
`http://agent.local` must never leak into production cards or copyable URLs.

Test these through the public proxy:

```bash
curl -fsS https://service.example/.well-known/agent-card.json
curl -fsS https://service.example/health
```

Every displayed operation URL should resolve, carry exactly one configured
base-path prefix, and return the same schema/price advertised by the card.

## Protected operations

The storefront can explain that an offering uses x402, MPP, or SIWX. React
adapters can support generated wallet flows; portable server pages instead
provide schemas and cURL handoffs with no client JavaScript. It must not:

- expose a server wallet key or facilitator credential to the browser;
- imply that an unauthenticated `fetch()` can complete a protected operation;
- treat “wallet connected” as payment approval;
- promise usage metering when the operation has a fixed price; or
- display a registration as proof that an output will be correct.

Browser input, output, task access tokens, and payment credentials should stay
out of local storage. Apply a strict content-security policy and render model
output as untrusted content.

## Verify the page

1. Add one free and one priced capability with input/output schemas.
2. Load the storefront on desktop and mobile.
3. In Next/TanStack, confirm free invoke works and protected invoke shows the
   correct protocol/price rather than silently failing. In Hono/Express,
   confirm there are no invoke controls or scripts.
4. Change a capability price and verify the UI updates from the card without a
   second hard-coded catalog.
5. Disable the Lucid task runtime and verify task controls disappear.
6. Break health deliberately and confirm the status becomes degraded/offline
   without hiding the public contract.
7. Run the generated matrix for all three presets and verify the three
   Cloudflare previews when repository credentials are configured.

## Production boundary

Reconciliation, payment analytics, alerts, task administration, tenant data,
and refunds belong in authenticated operational tooling. Do not add them to the
public storefront merely because they are convenient to render.

Continue with [Publish a catalog](/docs/build/catalog),
[deployment runbooks](/docs/operate/deploy), and the
[security threat model](/docs/operate/security).
